description: The 13 classes of data controllers required to register with the Commissioner under the Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Controllers) Order 2013, as amended by the Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Controllers) (Amendment) Order 2016
orderTitle: Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Controllers) Order 2013
amendmentOrderTitle: Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Controllers) (Amendment) Order 2016

classes.name: Communications
classes.description: Licensees under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588), including network facilities providers, network service providers, applications service providers, and content applications service providers
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Subscriber name and identity card number
keyComplianceChallenges: Processing of large volumes of sensitive location data
Lawful interception requirements under national security laws creating tension with PDPA
Cross-border data flows for roaming and international connectivity
Retention of call records for law enforcement purposes vs. PDPA retention principle

classes.name: Banking and Financial Institutions
classes.description: Licensed banks, investment banks, Islamic banks, development financial institutions, and other institutions licensed or registered under financial services legislation regulated by Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM)
examples: Development financial institutions (e.g., Bank Simpanan Nasional, Agrobank)
Operator of payment systems licensed by BNM
regulatoryOverlap: Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Credit history and credit scores (via CCRIS and CTOS)
keyComplianceChallenges: Mandatory KYC/AML reporting requirements creating conflicts with consent principles
Credit bureau reporting (CCRIS) — disclosure to third parties without data subject consent
Long retention periods mandated by BNM vs. PDPA retention principle
Correspondent banking relationships requiring cross-border data transfers

classes.name: Insurance
classes.description: Licensed insurers, takaful operators, and insurance intermediaries (agents, brokers, adjusters) regulated by Bank Negara Malaysia under financial services legislation
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Health and medical data for underwriting
Claims data including accident and injury details
keyComplianceChallenges: Processing of sensitive health data for underwriting creates high compliance risk
Third-party claims assessment requiring disclosure to loss adjusters and medical practitioners
Reinsurance arrangements requiring cross-border transfer of personal data
Anti-fraud data sharing between insurers

classes.name: Health
classes.description: Persons or organizations providing health care services, including registered medical practitioners, dentists, pharmacists, and operators of private healthcare facilities
examples: Medical and dental software providers (acting as data controllers)
regulatoryOverlap: Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act 1998 (Act 586)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Patient name, NRIC, and contact details
Next of kin and emergency contact information
keyComplianceChallenges: All health data is sensitive personal data under s29 — highest protection standards apply
Sharing patient data with insurance companies for claims requires careful consent management
Medical research using patient records — consent vs. public interest considerations
Telehealth services raise security and cross-border transfer considerations
Integration with national electronic health record systems

classes.name: Tourism and Hospitalities
classes.description: Licensed hotels, travel agencies, and tourism service providers registered under tourism-related legislation
examples: Hotels (including boutique hotels and serviced apartments)
regulatoryOverlap: Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture Malaysia
Hotels (Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur) Act 1954
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Guest name, NRIC/passport, and contact details
Visa and immigration information for international guests
keyComplianceChallenges: Guest register requirements — mandatory disclosure to authorities under Tourism Act
Cross-border transfer of guest data through global hotel reservation systems
Direct marketing and loyalty programme data retention
Third-party booking platforms acting as data processors

classes.name: Transport
classes.description: Licensed public transport operators including airlines, rail operators, bus companies, and ferry operators operating scheduled services within or to/from Malaysia
examples: Taxi and e-hailing platform operators (acting as data controllers)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: CCTV footage from vehicles and terminals
keyComplianceChallenges: Mandatory sharing of PNR data with authorities under international aviation agreements (ICAO Annex 9)
Cross-border data transfers inherent in international transport operations
Real-time tracking data from connected vehicles and GPS systems
CCTV data retention for security purposes

classes.name: Education
classes.description: Private educational institutions at all levels, including private schools, tuition centres, colleges, and private universities registered under education legislation
examples: Online learning platforms registered in Malaysia
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Student personal details and identity documents
Financial records and fee payment history
Medical and health records (for school health programmes)
keyComplianceChallenges: Processing of minors' personal data — heightened consent and protection requirements
Sharing student data with parents who are not the primary data subject (for adult students)
Alumni records and long-term data retention practices
Student learning analytics and profiling in digital learning environments

classes.name: Direct Selling
classes.description: Direct sales companies and distributors licensed under the Direct Sales and Anti-Pyramid Scheme Act 1993, including multi-level marketing (MLM) organizations
regulatoryOverlap: Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN)
Direct Sales and Anti-Pyramid Scheme Act 1993 (Act 500)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Bank account information for commission payments
keyComplianceChallenges: High-volume direct marketing creating significant compliance obligations under s43 opt-out
Sharing of personal data within multi-level distributor networks
Recruitment data used for both distributor acquisition and sales targeting
Cross-border operations in direct selling networks with regional headquarters

classes.name: Services
classes.description: Licensed professional service providers including legal, audit, accountancy, engineering, and architecture firms registered with their respective professional bodies
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Due diligence and background check information
keyComplianceChallenges: Legal professional privilege intersecting with PDPA access rights
KYC/AML obligations for lawyers and accountants creating mandatory disclosure conflicts
Long file retention practices in legal and professional contexts vs. retention principle
Processing of client commercially sensitive information

classes.name: Real Estate
classes.description: Licensed real estate agents, real estate negotiators, property developers, and real estate management companies registered with the Board of Valuers, Appraisers, Estate Agents and Property Managers (BOVEAP)
examples: Real estate investment trusts (REITs) in their data controller capacity
regulatoryOverlap: Board of Valuers, Appraisers, Estate Agents and Property Managers (BOVEAP)
keyComplianceChallenges: Sharing buyer information with property developers, lawyers, and banks in transactions
Tenant screening involving sensitive financial and reference data
CCTV systems in common areas of strata developments
Marketing of property to prospective buyers without prior relationship

classes.name: Utilities
classes.description: Licensed electricity supply companies and water operators providing utilities services to consumers in Malaysia
examples: Independent power producers (in their data controller capacity)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Meter serial numbers and installation details
keyComplianceChallenges: Smart meter data contains detailed patterns of daily life — high sensitivity
Mandatory reporting of consumption data to Energy Commission and SPAN
Debt recovery procedures involving credit reporting agencies
Use of consumption data for demand management and profiling programs

classes.name: Pawnbroking
classes.description: Licensed pawnbrokers operating under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972
examples: Ar-Rahn counters operated by financial cooperatives
regulatoryOverlap: Ministry of Urban Wellbeing, Housing and Local Government
Koperasi — cooperative pawnbrokers regulated by the Cooperatives Commission
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Loan amount, interest, and redemption records
Fingerprint records (where biometric authentication is used)
keyComplianceChallenges: Biometric data (fingerprints) constitutes sensitive personal data requiring highest protection
Mandatory retention of pawnbroking records under the Pawnbrokers Act vs. PDPA retention principle
Access by police to records — mandatory disclosure conflicting with PDPA principles
Handling of personal data of financially vulnerable customers

classes.name: Moneylending
classes.description: Licensed moneylenders under the Moneylenders Act 1951
examples: Credit cooperatives registered under the Cooperatives Act
Salary advance and payroll lending service providers licensed as moneylenders
regulatoryOverlap: Ministry of Urban Wellbeing, Housing and Local Government
Perbadanan PR1MA Malaysia (for housing loan schemes)
typicalPersonalDataProcessed: Loan application, disbursement, and repayment records
keyComplianceChallenges: Collection of guarantor data — third-party personal data requiring separate consent
Debt collection practices raising concerns about excessive data use and harassment
Credit scoring and profiling of applicants
Sharing with credit reporting agencies for default reporting
